title: DSC Holdings, AI Implementation Infrastructure for China's Used-Car Industry, Files for Nasdaq Listing
author: Deep Blue Finance
description: DaSouChe Holdings Ltd. (DSC) filed its Nasdaq registration statement on May 26, 2026, underwritten by Deutsche Bank, CICC, China Renaissance, and ICBC. The company reported declining revenue in 2025 (RMB 677 million vs. RMB 948 million in 2024) due to divestiture of its B2B financial product referral business, though net losses narrowed year-over-year to RMB 94.6 million. DSC positions itself as AI application infrastructure for China's used-car industry.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as underwriter for DSC's Nasdaq listing. Neutral sentiment as underwriting activity is routine business with no indication of positive or negative implications.

title: Electrolux Group publishes prospectus for the Rights Issue
author: Prnewswire
description: Electrolux Group announced the approval of its prospectus by the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority for a fully underwritten rights issue of approximately SEK 9 billion. The rights issue was approved by an Extraordinary General Meeting on May 27, 2026, with Morgan Stanley and SEB acting as Joint Global Coordinators and Deutsche Bank as Co-Bookrunner.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Morgan Stanley's role as Joint Global Coordinator is a routine advisory engagement that generates fees but provides no indication of positive or negative sentiment regarding the underwriting mandate itself.

title: Municipality Finance issues USD 1 billion benchmark under its MTN programme
author: Municipality Finance Plc
description: Municipality Finance Plc issued a USD 1 billion benchmark bond maturing on 27 May 2031 with a fixed interest rate of 4.250% per annum under its EUR 50 billion MTN programme. The bond was admitted to trading on Helsinki Stock Exchange on 27 May 2026, with Bank of Montreal Europe, BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank, and TD Global Finance serving as joint lead managers.

title: Electrolux Group announces terms for the fully underwritten rights issue of SEK 9,062 million
author: Electrolux Group
description: Electrolux Group announced the terms for a fully underwritten rights issue of approximately SEK 9.062 billion to finance profitable growth initiatives, strengthen its balance sheet, and support a strategic partnership with Midea Group in North America. The subscription price is set at SEK 16.75 per share, with existing shareholders receiving two subscription rights per share. Investor AB, the largest shareholder, has committed to subscribe for 37.56% of the issue, while Morgan Stanley, SEB, and Deutsche Bank provide full underwriting coverage.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is successfully raising SEK 9.062 billion through a fully underwritten rights issue to fund strategic growth initiatives, establish a partnership with Midea Group, optimize manufacturing operations, and strengthen its balance sheet. The full underwriting support and backing from major shareholder Investor AB indicate confidence in the company's strategic direction.

As Electrolux's largest shareholder, Investor AB is demonstrating strong confidence by committing to subscribe for 37.56% of the rights issue and providing guarantee undertakings, indicating belief in the company's strategic initiatives and future prospects.

title: Why Rocket Lab Stock Is Rocketing Higher This Week
author: Scott Levine
description: Rocket Lab stock surged to an all-time high of $133.18, rising 25.7% in early May following strong Q1 2026 results that beat analyst expectations with $200.4 million in revenue. Deutsche Bank raised its price target 64% to $120, while Clear Street initiated coverage with a $150 target. However, the stock trades at a steep 108x sales multiple, significantly above its historical 14.6x average.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 2026 financial results exceeded analyst expectations ($200.4M vs $189.7M consensus), stock reached all-time high, multiple analyst upgrades with raised price targets, and positive Q2 2026 guidance. However, sentiment is tempered by concerns about elevated valuation multiples (108x sales vs historical 14.6x average).

Mentioned as analyst firm raising price target on Rocket Lab, indicating positive outlook on the space sector, but this is analyst commentary rather than company-specific news affecting Deutsche Bank itself.
